source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList 
We’re solving some of the hardest problems in venture capital and private markets. You’ll work with a team that values precision, urgency, and long-term thinking. If you want to shape how startups are funded and built, this is the place.
About AngelList
We exist to accelerate innovation by increasing the number of successful startups in the world. We do this by building the financial infrastructure that makes it easier for more people to invest in world-changing companies.
AngelList is the nexus of venture capital and the startup community. We support $171B+ in assets and have powered investments into over 13,000 startups—over 300 of which are unicorns. Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role:
 
AngelList is seeking an Account Executive to join our team. As an Account Executive you will source, qualify and close venture & private equity funds for AngelList Fund Management or Software Suite. 
 
This role provides a unique opportunity to work alongside experienced leaders in the private market industry and shape AngelList’s sales strategy in this next phase of our growth. Given AngelList’s easy-to-use and scalable solution, you will have the opportunity to have an outsized impact not just on our company, but on the private market industry as a whole. 
 
You will manage the entire sales cycle, including creating and executing on outbound processes to close a pipeline of customers. As an early member of the sales team, you’ll be critical in helping us iterate on our pitch and processes as we scale. Internally, you will collaborate with AngelList’s product, operations, and legal teams to ensure a smooth experience for our users.
 
We are a fast-growing company, so we need Venture Account Executives who will take initiative, learn our product quickly and thoroughly, and bring a growth mindset to their work each day.

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList 
We’re solving some of the hardest problems in venture capital and private markets. You’ll work with a team that values precision, urgency, and long-term thinking. If you want to shape how startups are funded and built, this is the place.

Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role
$15.4 billion. 20,000 startups. That's the capital our platform has put to work — and every dollar of it runs through infrastructure your team will own.
We're looking for an Engineering Manager to lead our Accounting engineering team: the engine room that keeps our entire financial platform clean, accurate, and battle-tested. This isn't a maintenance role. It's a builder role — one where AI-first thinking, financial rigor, and technical leadership converge to reshape how venture capital actually moves.
If the math behind money excites you, and you want your engineering work to directly fuel the next generation of startups — this is the role.

source_jobs.title: Founding Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList 
We’re solving some of the hardest problems in venture capital and private markets. You’ll work with a team that values precision, urgency, and long-term thinking. If you want to shape how startups are funded and built, this is the place.
About AngelList
We exist to accelerate innovation by increasing the number of successful startups in the world. We do this by building the financial infrastructure that makes it easier for more people to invest in world-changing companies.
AngelList is the nexus of venture capital and the startup community. We support $171B+ in assets and have powered investments into over 13,000 startups—over 300 of which are unicorns. Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role:
Meridian is AngelList’s wealth platform for private markets. Meridian is building wealth products for the growing class of self-directed investors who want unique and direct access to private markets.
Built on more than a decade of AngelList innovation, Meridian extends the platform that already supports founders and fund managers to serve investors now. Meridian is early, and this role presents an opportunity to help shape the next generation of products for private market investing.
As a Senior Software Engineer on Meridian, you’ll own the technical design and delivery of major product surfaces across investing and portfolio management, from early architecture and sequencing tradeoffs through launch and iteration. This is a founding engineer role on a zero-to-one product with meaningful distribution, regulatory complexity, and infrastructure leverage. You’ll work closely with product, design, compliance, and supply to shape what private market investing feels like for the next decade.
We're also hiring a Founding Engineer for our new USVC product, and we will consider you for both.

source_jobs.title: Head of AML
source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList 
We’re solving some of the hardest problems in venture capital and private markets. You’ll work with a team that values precision, urgency, and long-term thinking. If you want to shape how startups are funded and built, this is the place.
About AngelList
We exist to accelerate innovation by increasing the number of successful startups in the world. We do this by building the financial infrastructure that makes it easier for more people to invest in world-changing companies.
AngelList is the nexus of venture capital and the startup community. We support $171B+ in assets and have powered investments into over 13,000 startups—over 300 of which are unicorns. Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role
AngelList is hiring a Head of Anti-Money Laundering to join our Compliance team in New York or San Francisco (hybrid). This role sits at the intersection of fintech innovation and regulatory integrity. You'll own the AML program and compliance relationship with our banking partners end-to-end, keeping our partner network healthy and our programs exam-ready as we scale.
Day-to-day, you'll serve as the go-to compliance contact for the banks we work with, building real trust with our counterparts. Internally, you'll work closely with our KYC/TM Ops, Product, and Engineering teams optimizing process and systems. You'll also be a core contributor to the compliance buildout for Nova, meaning your fingerprints will be on something new, not just something inherited. What makes this role rare: you're not joining a large bank compliance department where you're one of fifty. You're the person. Partners will know your name, and what you build here will actually matter.
If you're energized by the idea of owning something important at a company that moves fast and takes compliance as a competitive advantage, this is your seat. You'll get real scope, real relationships, and a front-row view of how fintech compliance gets built right.

source_jobs.title: Head of Data
source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList 
We’re solving some of the hardest problems in venture capital and private markets. You’ll work with a team that values precision, urgency, and long-term thinking. If you want to shape how startups are funded and built, this is the place.
About AngelList
We exist to accelerate innovation by increasing the number of successful startups in the world. We do this by building the financial infrastructure that makes it easier for more people to invest in world-changing companies.
AngelList is the nexus of venture capital and the startup community. We support $171B+ in assets and have powered investments into over 13,000 startups—over 300 of which are unicorns. Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role
We're hiring a Head of Data to turn AngelList's data into an unfair advantage.
AngelList sits in a rare position. We support $171B in assets across 25,000+ funds and syndicates, with $80B+ moved across our banking infrastructure. 80% of the top 10 2025 Midas List VCs invest in funds on AngelList. Our network spans 2,300+ GPs and 72,000+ LPs, the largest in private markets, and we have over a decade of venture fund transaction data underneath it. We've reached $100M+ ARR with essentially zero marketing spend and we're ready to invest. The business is unusual: a marketplace, an investing platform, a banking partner, and a software + services business, all reinforcing each other. The data implications are extraordinary, and most of the interesting questions are still unanswered.
Those questions are the work. How do the business units actually compound on each other? What's the right attribution model when one product introduces a customer to another? What does incrementality look like when our channels are dominated by network and reputation rather than paid acquisition? What's the LTV of a fund manager vs. an LP vs. a banking customer, and how should that change where we invest?
Our data team spent the last year earning trust in the numbers: Snowflake live, pipelines reliable, a shared source of truth. That foundation is mostly there. What we want next is the layer on top: attribution we trust, incrementality we can measure, segmentations and forecasts that change how we invest, experimentation as default. You'll lead a small, senior team of three to start, and personally do a meaningful share of the work and shaping of the team. For a growth-focused data scientist ready to do both, there aren't many roles like it.

source_jobs.title: Investment Operations Associate
source_jobs.description:
About USVC
We’re on a mission to allow everyone to own a stake in the future being built by private technology companies.
For decades, only a select few well-connected participants could access America's most promising private companies. That changes with USVC: a new SEC-registered fund by AngelList Asset Management that broadens access to venture capital, allowing retail investors to gain exposure to promising companies before they become well-known. USVC leverages AngelList’s network of 4,500+ active managers running 25,000+ funds and backing 13,000+ active startups to access exposure to tomorrow’s defining companies


About the Role:
This is a rare opportunity to reshape how the public invests in innovation. You'll come in at the ground level to help build a world-class operations function at the forefront of venture and fintech, backed by AngelList's platform and reputation. You will own transforming investment decisions into closed positions. You will support USVC and a growing range of co-invest vehicles, accounts, and funds as our asset management business scales. This role owns the process that scales expanding private market access.

source_jobs.title: Investor Services Specialist
source_jobs.description:
About USVC
We’re on a mission to allow everyone to own a stake in the future being built by private technology companies.
For decades, only a select few well-connected participants could access America's most promising private companies. That changes with USVC: a new SEC-registered fund by AngelList Asset Management that broadens access to venture capital, allowing retail investors to gain exposure to promising companies before they become well-known. USVC leverages AngelList’s network of 4,500+ active managers running 25,000+ funds and backing 13,000+ active startups to access exposure to tomorrow’s defining companies


About the Role:
USVC is seeking an Investor Services Specialist to own the self-directed investor experience. This supports every retail investor who puts capital in through usvc.com, from their first KYC check through their tenth reinvestment.
This is a rare opportunity to own the frontline of how the public invests in venture capital. You're the primary point of contact for USVC's investors and will build a support function from the ground floor into autonomous systems that scale with USVC's growth.

source_jobs.title: Limited Partner Relations Associate
source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList 
We’re solving some of the hardest problems in venture capital and private markets. You’ll work with a team that values precision, urgency, and long-term thinking. If you want to shape how startups are funded and built, this is the place.
About AngelList
We exist to accelerate innovation by increasing the number of successful startups in the world. We do this by building the financial infrastructure that makes it easier for more people to invest in world-changing companies.
AngelList is the nexus of venture capital and the startup community. We support $171B+ in assets and have powered investments into over 13,000 startups—over 300 of which are unicorns. Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role:
When a GP raises a fund on AngelList, LPs trust the platform as much as they trust the GP. LP Relations is the team behind that trust: the front line for more than 100,000 investors, especially in the moments that are hardest to get right. It comes down to the details: clear communication, accurate reporting, document management, correct money movement, and capable support when questions come up.
This is a customer-facing role. You’ll work directly with the investors in the funds we administer and run point on the toughest situations: diligence calls with fund counsel, frustrated GPs, time-sensitive closings, and edge cases that have to be solved in real time. Resolving them means pulling in the right people across legal, tax, banking, sales, and product.
The strongest people on this team notice the patterns behind recurring issues and fix the system. You will surface what’s broken, form a point of view on how it should work, and drive the projects that make our service better for every LP. This is a role for someone who operates with judgment and ownership, and who is energized by problems that don’t come with a playbook.
This is a hybrid role based in our NYC office. We expect you in the office 2-3 days a week.

source_jobs.title: Marketing Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList 
We’re solving some of the hardest problems in venture capital and private markets. You’ll work with a team that values precision, urgency, and long-term thinking. If you want to shape how startups are funded and built, this is the place.
About AngelList
We exist to accelerate innovation by increasing the number of successful startups in the world. We do this by building the financial infrastructure that makes it easier for more people to invest in world-changing companies.
AngelList is the nexus of venture capital and the startup community. We support $171B+ in assets and have powered investments into over 13,000 startups—over 300 of which are unicorns. Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role:
This is a chance to rebuild marketing ops from first principles, designed around what AI makes possible.
We need a clearer perspective on where to invest marketing dollars and why. This role exists to build that clarity. You'll own the marketing tech stack, full-funnel reporting, and market intelligence insights that guide where dollars go. You'll be the natural partner to marketers running experiments: the source of truth who provides data backing and builds the instrumentation (dashboards, signals, measurement systems) that tells us what's working and what we're missing.
This role requires wide altitude coverage: executive-level strategy partnership and hands-on technical execution. You'll sit at the intersection of marketing, sales, and executive leadership. You’ll frame what the numbers mean and what we should do about them. Hands-on execution is required because the person making strategic recommendations is the same person who understands exactly how the data is built, where it breaks, and what it can't tell you. The right candidate is comfortable moving between these modes daily.

source_jobs.title: Principal Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList

Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role:
 The Principal Recruiter will support AngelList’s expanding business and strategic hiring goals. The right person will be a creative and adaptable generalist, capable of earning trust and delivering successful hires for key roles across the company. Instead of focusing on a single domain (or even isolating in technical or GTM roles), the Principal Recruiter will operate function-agnostic, and should be comfortable learning about every part of the business, ramping on a broad range of roles, and partnering with all executive team members. It will be a high-impact, high-exposure role, working closely with AngelList’s leadership.
At AngelList, our mission is to accelerate innovation by empowering startups and the people who build them. And on our team, that starts with recruiting high-agency and high-curiosity builders to accelerate problem solving and business growth.
This job isn't just about hiring — it's about putting the right people in the right seats so teams can outperform their goals.

source_jobs.description:

Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role:
AngelList is hiring a Product Counsel to sit at the intersection of law, product, and private markets innovation. Legal architecture isn't a constraint here — it's a competitive advantage.
You'll be embedded across product, engineering, and business teams, working on tightly regulated, zero-margin-for-error infrastructure: fund formation, banking infrastructure, investor onboarding, capital calls, and data systems. You'll help shape the regulatory playbook for the next generation of private markets. This role reports directly to the General Counsel of Product.
Most legal teams at financial companies are built to say no. Ours is built to find the yes — and then build the structure that makes it safe, scalable, and replicable. If you're energized by complexity, frustrated by boilerplate, and want your legal work to directly shape products used by thousands of GPs, LPs, and founders, this is the role.

source_jobs.title: Product Designer,  Funds
source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList 
We’re solving some of the hardest problems in venture capital and private markets. You’ll work with a team that values precision, urgency, and long-term thinking. If you want to shape how startups are funded and built, this is the place.

Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role: 
Our Funds team is focused on two goals: (1) driving invested capital volume by delivering products for larger, more complex customers, and (2) raising the quality bar for our existing full-service fund management platform.
As a Product Designer on this team, you’ll play a central role in rearchitecting our existing software into deeply configurable accounting, intelligence, and investor management systems that can scale with institutional needs. You’ll also help us apply AI agents to transform service-heavy back office workflows into software and build entirely new categories of products for fund managers.
You’ll enjoy this role if you thrive in ambiguity, love designing system-level solutions, and are motivated by building private markets infrastructure that drives capital to entrepreneurs building our future.

source_jobs.title: Product Manager, Funds
source_jobs.description:

Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About this Role
 Running a venture fund today requires a team of accountants, analysts, lawyers, and operators. The future is a headless, agentic experience where software runs the back office and the General Partner (GP) focuses on raising capital and picking winners.

We’re hiring a product manager to build this future.

You'll own the strategy, roadmap, and outcomes for Full Service, our flagship product for emerging managers, and Scout Funds, the product that puts you in the room with some of the largest names in venture, with the ultimate goal of increasing our market share.

If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role:
Built for the growing class of self-directed investors who want direct access to private market opportunities, Meridian is AngelList's wealth platform for private markets. Extending more than a decade of AngelList infrastructure, Meridian is the opportunity to serve investors directly. The product is early, and this role will shape the next generation of products for private market investing.

As the Founding Product Manager, you'll own core investor-facing product areas end-to-end — from onboarding and investing to portfolio insights and reporting. You'll work from first principles to define the modern wealth platform for private markets, turning customer insight, data, and regulatory constraints into elegant, high-trust experiences in a fast-moving, regulated space.

This is a senior IC role for someone who wants to build the product, not direct a team that builds it. If you've owned a complex, customer-facing product line end-to-end and want the next one to define a category, keep reading.

If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role:
We’re building the most efficient platform for operating funds and investing in startups. Our Infrastructure team is small, fast-moving, and foundational to every product we build. As a Senior Infrastructure Engineer, you will shape the platform infrastructure that supports billions in venture assets.
This isn’t a pure operations role. You will be building: developer platforms, deployment pipelines, infrastructure automation, and foundational systems that make our engineering team fast and reliable. Our stack runs on Kubernetes and AWS, and we use tools like Pulumi, cdk8s, and ArgoCD — but what matters most is your ability to design, build, and iterate quickly.

source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList 
We’re solving some of the hardest problems in venture capital and private markets. You’ll work with a team that values precision, urgency, and long-term thinking. If you want to shape how startups are funded and built, this is the place.

Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role
AngelList manages $171B+ in assets and facilitating investments across over 13k startups. At the core of that is our banking infrastructure: the systems that move money, manage accounts, and ensure every dollar is tracked with precision.
We're looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our Banking team. This team builds the foundational components and services that other engineering teams across AngelList depend on—account primitives, transaction pipelines, ledger systems, and banking integrations. You won't just be building features; you'll be building the platform that features are built on.
This is a high-leverage role. The systems you design will be consumed by teams across AngelList and serve as the core of a high-growth new product vertical. You'll need to think in terms of contracts, reliability, and extensibility—balancing the rigor required of financial infrastructure with the pace of a company that ships fast with a lean team.

AngelList is hiring a Senior Product Engineer to continue to build one of our most loved products – "Investor Management," which includes Digital Subscriptions and Data Room. Unifying investor workflows within a single platform has result in a 10x better experience for fund managers and their investors.
At AngelList, we pride ourselves on having an outsized impact on the venture capital industry. Teams are explicitly small and built on high-autonomy, ownership, collaboration, and mutual respect. The team is located in our San Francisco HQ, working with other thought partners to solve hard problems and drive innovation forward.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Accounting
source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList 
We’re solving some of the hardest problems in venture capital and private markets. You’ll work with a team that values precision, urgency, and long-term thinking. If you want to shape how startups are funded and built, this is the place.

AngelList is the nexus of venture capital and the startup community. We support $171B+ in assets and have powered investments into over 13,000 startups—over 300 of which are unicorns. Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role:
AngelList helps over 25,000 funds manage $171B+ in startup investments. Our software automates the work for our in-house team of operators, financial accountants, and tax professionals. We are now charting the course to managing $1T in investments. 
AngelList aims to power the best-performing venture funds. We can win these customers if we deliver financials more accurately and more quickly than anyone else. We need to issue and collect capital calls faster than anyone. We need to make our customers’ requests either self-serve, or so operationally tight behind-the-scenes, that it feels self-serve. We will get there by delivering a new product experience, where the GP may self-serve more of their requests; and by overhauling our accounting system to automate more operational work.

source_jobs.title: Strategic Finance Lead, Funds
source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList

Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role:
AngelList’s Funds business moves capital to startups at scale. The hardest ongoing question is how to deploy resources across go-to-market, product, engineering, customer relations, and operations. We’re looking for someone who will operate with the autonomy and scope of a division CFO, and your fingerprints should be visible on the decisions made, investments pursued, and outcomes achieved.
As a member of the Funds leadership team, you will own the central planning model that sets growth targets and informs where we invest. You'll work with leaders across the business to find opportunities, evaluate investments, and build conviction on where to invest next. Pricing and packaging are yours, and so is the question of where the money is made - including unit economics across customers and product lines.
This is not a traditional finance role. Success is whether the business makes better decisions and accelerates growth. You'll form a point of view quickly, navigate ambiguity, and drive alignment across a talented, opinionated leadership team.

source_jobs.title: Venture Relations Associate
source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList 
We’re solving some of the hardest problems in venture capital and private markets. You’ll work with a team that values precision, urgency, and long-term thinking. If you want to shape how startups are funded and built, this is the place.
About AngelList
We exist to accelerate innovation by increasing the number of successful startups in the world. We do this by building the financial infrastructure that makes it easier for more people to invest in world-changing companies.
AngelList is the nexus of venture capital and the startup community. We support $171B+ in assets and have powered investments into over 13,000 startups—over 300 of which are unicorns. Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role:
Our Venture Relations team at AngelList serves as the main point of contact for our clients across our suite of private market software products. Our clients include some of the most exciting investors in the Venture Capital ecosystem, CFOs of multi-billion dollar private market funds, and operators within private fund management. The Venture Relations team partners with our clients to deliver a delightful product experience, drive retention, and enable growth.
In this role, you’ll serve as the operational center of excellence for AngelList’s customers by working closely with Account Managers to manage fund operations, troubleshoot client issues, and serve as subject matter expert on our software suite. You will work directly with General Partners, CFOs, and other external stakeholders to drive outcomes and focus on executing critical workflows to provide Account Managers and our customers with increased leverage. Internally, you will collaborate with sales, engineering, legal, finance, operations, and tax teams to enhance platform efficiency and track client challenges. 
Please note, this is not an investing role. This is a customer facing role.
This role will be a hybrid role in our NYC office only - expectations are that you are in the office 2-3 days a week.

source_jobs.title: Workplace & Employee Experience Lead
source_jobs.description:
Why Join AngelList 
We’re solving some of the hardest problems in venture capital and private markets. You’ll work with a team that values precision, urgency, and long-term thinking. If you want to shape how startups are funded and built, this is the place.
About AngelList
We exist to accelerate innovation by increasing the number of successful startups in the world. We do this by building the financial infrastructure that makes it easier for more people to invest in world-changing companies.
AngelList is the nexus of venture capital and the startup community. We support $171B+ in assets and have powered investments into over 13,000 startups—over 300 of which are unicorns. Today, 57% of top-tier U.S. VC deals involve investors on AngelList. While our scale is large, our ambition is larger.
If you're excited to build the future of private markets, come build with us.


About the Role
We believe in-person time is one of the most powerful tools we have for accelerating company priorities. Thoughtful in-person experiences deepen relationships and create the kind of learning that's harder to replicate async. And when you have an office, you can make it something more: a physical home for great work.
We are hiring a Senior IC to own both the SF hub and the programs that make in-person time worth it for the whole company: offsites, travel, onboarding weeks, and the recurring rituals that build trust across a distributed team. In-person time is part of how we execute: when we gather, it should accelerate a real company priority, and you should be able to articulate what it was meant to unlock and what changed because it happened.
This role is stewardship with impact. You care about the details in the space and the tone of a room. You think about atmosphere, energy, and quality. At the same time, you operate with rigor. You close loops. You build systems. You make sure great experiences are repeatable, not accidental.

notes: AngelList is a financial infrastructure company that supports $171B+ in assets and has a strong presence in the venture capital and startup community.
